body.body:
From D117 (closed as ticketed). The environment-tree-in-DB work is mostly done — SYSCFG nodes, SWARM nodes, env_sync, and machine_manager.py (#342, the DB-backed replacement) are all in place. The remaining piece is finishing the machines.json removal so D117's 'machines.json becomes seed file not source of truth' actually holds.

## Current dual-write situation

- machine_manager.py is the canonical DB-backed registry (its docstring says 'Replaces machines.json')
- BUT main.py:1331-1336 still reads/writes machines.json on first-boot self-registration
- AND push_sources.py:46 still defines MACHINES_JSON = paths().machines_json with a MachinesWatcher that watches the file

Meaning: writes happen to both the DB and the file, reads sometimes hit the file. Drift is possible if anything updates one and not the other.

## Pick one of two endings

**Option A: machines.json becomes seed-only.** It exists as a starter file for fresh boxes that have no DB yet. On first boot, machine_manager loads it once, populates the SWARM_* + machine config nodes, then never reads it again. machine_manager becomes the only writer. main.py + push_sources.py stop touching the file.

**Option B: delete machines.json entirely.** First-boot bootstraps from a hardcoded default machine config or from environment variables. The file simply doesn't exist anywhere. machine_manager owns everything.

Akien picks. I lean toward Option A because it gives a human-editable starting point for new boxes without needing DB access first. Option B is cleaner but loses the bootstrap convenience.

## Concrete work

1. Pick A or B (Akien decision before sprint)
2. main.py:1331-1336 — remove the machines.json self-registration block. Self-registration goes through machine_manager → SWARM_* node directly.
3. push_sources.py:46 + MachinesWatcher — either repoint to watch the SWARM nodes via cortex change events, or delete the watcher entirely if A is chosen and the file is read once at boot.
4. main.py:733 — first-boot announcement path that mentions self-registering in machines.json — update narrative.
5. If Option B: delete the file from the runtime instance dir + remove paths().machines_json from PathManager.
6. Verify: no remaining references to machines.json across wild_igor/. Test the first-boot path on a fresh instance.

## Existing work to check

- wild_igor/igor/cognition/machine_manager.py (#342) — the canonical DB-backed registry
- wild_igor/igor/main.py:1306-1336 — first-boot announcement + self-register block
- wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources.py:46, 719 — MACHINES_JSON + MachinesWatcher
- wild_igor/igor/paths.py:83 — paths().machines_json property
- D211 inference_rank and per-machine fields (in_use_hours, network_type, roles, aliases) — these are in machines.json today; verify machine_manager surfaces them all from the DB

## Why this matters

T-router-off-limits-violation (filed earlier today) is partly about machines knowing their own off-limits hours and the router respecting them. If config still lives in two places, the router might consult the wrong one. Closing this dual-write before sprinting the router fix is the right order.

## Provenance

D117 (Akien 2026-04-11, surfaced 2026-04-14, closed as ticketed). Related: D211 inference_rank, #342 DB-backed machine registry.
body.title: Finish phasing out machines.json — make it seed-only or delete
body.result: 26202e6a — machines.json is now a DB echo. machine_manager gains export_to_machines_json + register_self. main.py self-registration replaced with single machine_manager call. set_machine_override + clear_machine_override refresh the echo on every write. Existing readers untouched — they read a now-always-fresh echo. Verified live: 6 machines exported with _meta block pointing at Postgres as source of truth. 2209 tests pass. Per Akien 2026-04-14: 'the machines json is for me to read. it's an echo of the database, that's all.'
